title: Bridge Essay: The ethical significance of user-control in AI-driven speech-BCIs: a narrative r

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Inner speech, mantra meditation, focused attention, semantic cognition, verbal repetition.

**The ethical significance of user-control in AI-driven speech-BCIs: anarrative review.** — Front Hum Neurosci. PMID: 39006157. DOI: 10.3389/fnhum.2024.1420334. AI-driven brain-computed interfaces aimed at restoring speech for individuals living with locked-in-syndrome are paired with ethical implications for user'sautonomy, privacy and responsibility. Embedding options for sufficient levels of user-control in speech-BCI design has been proposed to mitigate these ethical challenges. However, how user-control in speech-BCIs is conceptualized and how it relates to these ethical challenges is underdetermined. In this narrative literature review, we aim to...
